semantic.role: CI/CD integration template for automated architecture compliance checks on pull requests
responsibilities: Defines a GitHub Actions workflow (.yml) that runs `spine sync` and `spine check` on PR events
Covers required environment variables (LLM_API_KEY), permissions, and Node.js setup
Posts a PR comment with violation details when architecture checks fail
Updates existing bot comments to avoid duplication
Fails the workflow if violations are detected
outOfScope: Detailed configuration of ArchSpine (e.g., .spine/config.json, language models)
Explanation of `spine sync` or `spine check` commands themselves

English.purpose: This document provides a ready-to-use GitHub Actions workflow that automatically enforces architectural rules on every pull request. It helps teams maintain a consistent architecture by running ArchSpine’s sync and check steps in CI.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for developers who have initialized ArchSpine in their repository and want to add an automated architecture gate to their PR workflow. Readers need basic familiarity with GitHub Actions and should have a valid LLM_API_KEY secret configured.
key_takeaways: The workflow triggers on pull request open, synchronize, and reopen events.
It runs `spine sync` to update the architecture model, then `spine check` to detect violations.
On failure, it posts a detailed comment on the PR (or updates an existing one) and fails the workflow.
Requires a secret named `LLM_API_KEY` set in the repository’s Actions settings.
